content.problemStatement:
In a Chess match "a + b", each player has a clock which shows aaa minutes at the start and whenever a player makes a move, bbb seconds are added to this player's clock. Time on a player's clock decreases during that player's turns and remains unchanged during the other player's turns. If the time on some player's clock hits zero (but not only in this case), this player loses the game.

There's a 3 + 2 blitz chess match. After NNN turns (i.e. ⌊N+12⌋\left\lfloor \frac{N+1}{2} \right\rfloor⌊2N+1​⌋ moves made by white and ⌊N2⌋\left\lfloor \frac{N}{2} \right\rfloor⌊2N​⌋ moves made by black), the game ends and the clocks of the two players stop; they show that the players (white and black) have AAA and BBB seconds left respectively. Note that after the NNN-th turn, b=2b = 2b=2 seconds are still added to the clock of the player that made the last move and then the game ends.

Find the total duration of the game, i.e. the number of seconds that have elapsed from the start of the game until the end.
constraints: If N is odd then A >= 2, if N is even then B >= 2
All given inputs correspond to at least one valid game
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers N, A and B.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with one integer – the duration of the game in seconds.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each case compute total time = 360 + 2*N and subtract A+B.
Case1: 360+20-2=378.
Case2: 360+22-3=379.
Case3: 360+24-384=0.

solution.approach: Each player starts with 180 seconds. After every move 2 seconds are added to the mover's clock. After N moves the total amount of time that has ever been on both clocks equals the initial total (2 * 180) plus the sum of all increments (2 * N). At the end the clocks show A and B seconds. The elapsed time is therefore (360 + 2*N) - (A + B). The formula is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); A = int(next(it)); B = int(next(it))
        ans = 360 + 2*N - (A + B)
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
